member.name: Fleur Anderson
member.synopsis: Fleur Anderson is the Labour MP for Putney , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Anglo Portuguese School of London: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to speed up the completion of building works at the Anglo Portuguese School of London; and if she will ensure that key facilities, including the play deck and kitchen, are co
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is aware of significant disruption for the Anglo Portuguese School of London and its pupils since the original contractor entered administration. A new contractor, Kier, was quickly appointed but identified various issues which needed to be...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Road Traffic Offences: Speed Limits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department has analysed international examples of requiring intervening Intelligent Speed Assistance technology for high-risk and repeat speeding offenders.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not undertaken specific analysis of international approaches to require intervening Intelligent Speed Assistance technology for high‑risk or repeat speeding offenders. The Government keeps the motoring offences framework under review, b...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Road Traffic Offences: Speed Limits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department has a national definition of a high-risk repeat speeding offender.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not undertaken specific analysis of international approaches to require intervening Intelligent Speed Assistance technology for high‑risk or repeat speeding offenders. The Government keeps the motoring offences framework under review, b...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Road Traffic Offences: Speed Limits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department has asked police forces to provide data on repeat speeding offenders to inform future road safety policy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not undertaken specific analysis of international approaches to require intervening Intelligent Speed Assistance technology for high‑risk or repeat speeding offenders. The Government keeps the motoring offences framework under review, b...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Animal Experiments: Dogs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the levels of the use of dogs at the MBR Acres facility in Cambridgeshire; and whether she plans to take steps to end the breeding and supply of dogs for scientific tes
writtenQuestions.answerText: Pharmaceutical legislation requires that, before a medicine can be approved for testing in humans, it is tested in a rodent and non-rodent species under international guidelines. This may include dogs as an appropriate model. The number of Beagles bred is...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Pancreatic Cancer: Medical Treatments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to improve access to treatments for pancreatic cancer and increase survival rates.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Cancer Plan includes further details on how we will improve outcomes for cancer patients, including those with rare and less survivable cancers such as pancreatic cancer, as well as speeding up diagnosis and treatment. It will help ensure pat...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Naloxone
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to expand access to naloxone in communities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Naloxone forms a key part of the Government’s plan to reduce drug related deaths, and we are committed to expanding access to naloxone in communities across the country. The Government has already amended the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 to expand acc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Brain: Tumours
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to help ensure the routine freezing of suitable brain tumour tissue samples to improve patient access to genomic testing, personalised treatments and research.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department continues to engage with NHS England and wider stakeholders on improving access to high-quality pathology and genomics services, including those relevant to brain tumour patients.NHS England and the Department will work with professional bo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Game: Birds
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment she has made of the environmental and animal welfare impacts of the breeding, release and shooting of game birds, and whether she plans to introduce a ban on these prac
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government has no plans to ban the breeding, release or shooting of gamebirds. However, gamebird shooting can have trade-offs with environmental, economic, and animal health and welfare outcomes and we want to ensure the highest standards of practice...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Palantir
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will review the NHS Federated Data Platform contract with Palantir ahead of the 2027 break clause, in the context of reported concerns about undisclosed access to identifiable patient data an
writtenQuestions.answerText: The NHS Federated Data Platform (NHS FDP) safely connects information from different systems across the National Health Service into a single, secure environment. This allows staff to co-ordinate care better to improve outcomes for patients. The NHS FDP...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pancreatic Cancer: Diagnosis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to improve early diagnosis of pancreatic cancer, and whether he is supporting the development of new scanning technologies or screening methods to detect pancrea
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to improving outcomes for people affected by pancreatic cancer through earlier diagnosis, faster treatment and increased access to innovation. The National Cancer Plan for England identifies earlier diagnosis as a key priority ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospital Beds
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the impact of corridor care and delayed transfers from emergency departments on patient safety, including the reported 16,000 deaths annually; and what steps his Depa
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government and NHS England are clear that corridor care is not acceptable and should never become standard practice. The figure of 16,000 deaths annually is a reported estimate rather than an official Government statistic. However, the Department reco...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Sudan: Non-governmental Organisations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what representations her Department has made to the Rapid Support Forces on their requirement for international NGOs to register in Darfur; and what steps she is taking to he
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government remains deeply concerned by the recent directives issued by the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) Humanitarian Agency, that requires humanitarian organisations, including the UN, to register their presence in areas of RSF-control or face expulsion...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Breasts: Plastic Surgery
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when the Government will publish its response to the Women and Equalities Committee’s report on the health impacts of breast implants and other cosmetic procedures.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the important work of the Women and Equalities Committee (WEC) and remains committed to addressing longstanding concerns around safety in the cosmetic sector.We will issue the Government’s response to the WEC report on cosmetic p...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Forests: Commodities
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her Department is taking to ensure that (a) the UK Forest Risk Commodities Regulations operate on a shared responsibility basis and (b) compliance costs are borne by downstr
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the urgency of taking action to ensure that UK consumption of forest risk commodities is not driving deforestation. The Government is currently considering its approach to addressing the impact of the use of forest risk commoditi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Craniocervical Instability
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to ensure that patients with suspected craniocervical instability in the context of hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos syndrome are not (a) misdiagnosed and (b) inappropriately manag
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the challenges faced by patients with hypermobile Ehlers‑Danlos syndromes (hEDS) and associated conditions, including suspected craniocervical instability (CCI), which can be complex to diagnose and manage.In England, decisions a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pigs: Slaughterhouses
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether she plans to launch a consultation on phasing out the use of carbon dioxide to slaughter pigs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in this Government’s animal welfare strategy, Defra will consult on banning carbon dioxide gas stunning of pigs and on possible timescales for phasing out this method. Further details will be set out later this year.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Gyms and Leisure: Business Rates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether she plans to extend business rates relief to independent gyms and fitness centres.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Pubs rents in business rates valuations are analysed differently to some other sectors. While most hospitality and leisure properties are valued by comparing the size of the property, pubs are valued by comparing their turnover potential. Industry bodies ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Sudan: Humanitarian Situation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, with reference to her Oral Statement of 5 February 2026 on Sudan, Official Report, column 438, what steps her Department is taking to strengthen closer work through local par
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the comments made by the Foreign Secretary in her article for the Daily Telegraph on 15 April marking the third anniversary of the war in Sudan, where she said:Amid all this horror, another Sudan endures, defined not by violence,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Craniocervical Instability: Surgery

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department recognises that people with Ehlers‑Danlos syndromes (EDS), hypermobility spectrum disorders (HSD), and related conditions such as postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome, mast cell activation disorders, myalgic encephalomyelitis, also kno...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

Name of donor: (1) Government of Jersey London Office (2) States of Guernsey (3) Commonwealth Parliamentary Association Address of donor: (1) 4th Floor, 2 Queen Anne’s Gate Buildings, Dartmouth Street, London SW1H 9BP (2) Sir Charles Frossard House, La Charroterie, St Peter Port, Guernsey, Channel Islands GY1 1FH (3) Richmond House, Houses of parliament, London SW1A 0AA Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): (1) On island transport and subsistence, value £505 (2) On island transport and subsistence, value £340 (3) Transport and accommodation, value £998.98 Destination of visit: Jersey and Guernsey (also visited Sark) Dates of visit: 26 March 2026 to 29 March 2026 Purpose of visit: Channel Islands All-Party Parliamentary Group visit to the Channel Islands. The group engaged in high level political meetings with Channel Islands Ministers, deputies and dignitaries and engaged in a wide array of cultural visits to increase understanding and cooperation between parliamentarians and their counterparts in the Channel Islands. Members met and engaged with representatives of the CPA in Jersey and Guernsey. (Registered 29 April 2026)
